label: Hospitals Damaged
note: Iran: 289 health facilities damaged (OCHA) — 50+ primary care centers + 5 hospitals closed — Lebanon: 4+ hospitals closed — WHO: 53 healthcare worker deaths, 117 injuries in 77 attacks
source: Iranian Health Ministry / WHO / OCHA / Lebanese MoH / ICRC
detail: OCHA (Mar 25): 289 health facilities damaged in Iran, 50+ primary healthcare centers and 5 hospitals closed. WHO: 53 healthcare worker deaths and 117 injuries in 77 attacks on healthcare. 87,000+ civilian units damaged (61,000 homes, 19,000 commercial). 600 schools damaged. 17 Red Crescent centers hit. Iran displacement: 3.2 million people (~600,000-1 million households). Lebanon: 1,200+ killed (107+ children), 3,000+ wounded, 1 million+ displaced. 472 schools used as shelters. ICRC: conflict has 'upended the lives of millions.'

label: Schools Destroyed
note: ~600 educational centers attacked per Iranian Red Crescent. Minab school (168 killed). Iran University of Science and Technology severely damaged. IRGC threatened retaliatory strikes on US/Israeli campuses in Qatar and UAE.
source: Iranian Red Crescent / AP / NYT / Amnesty
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval compound. Amnesty International verified 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) at Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school on February 28. US internal investigation (Mar 11) determined the strike was likely due to outdated DIA intelligence — the school was once part of an IRGC compound but had been walled off since 2016. Weapons experts confirmed Tomahawk missile fragments. UN investigation initiated March 17, ongoing. HRW and Amnesty demanded accountability for potential war crimes.

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: IEA: 44+ energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — worst since combined 1973 + 1979 oil shocks
source: IEA / DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: IEA (Mar 23): At least 44 energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — 'very severe, worse than the combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks.' IEA released record 400M barrels from strategic stockpiles (US: 172M from SPR). Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields (production collapsed from 4.2M to ~1.2-1.4M bpd). Combined Gulf production cuts exceed 10M bpd (JPMorgan). Iran: Kharg destroyed, South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 struck. Qatar: Ras Laffan 12.8 MTPA lost (17% of LNG exports, 3-5yr repair). SAMREF (Saudi) struck. Kuwait: Mina al-Ahmadi hit. Haifa BAZAN refinery. Bahrain Bapco under force majeure. Iran sanctions lifted on ~140M barrels at sea (30 days). Jones Act waived 60 days.

label: Nuclear Facilities Struck
note: Fordow, Isfahan UCF, Natanz (3x), Bushehr perimeter (3x — closest 350m from reactor), Arak Heavy Water (Shahid Khondab), Ardakan yellowcake — IAEA: Bushehr 'reddest line of all'
detail: Fordow enrichment facility struck Day 1 with B-2/GBU-57 MOPs. Isfahan UCF struck Day 1. Natanz struck Day 22. Day 29: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Ardakan yellowcake facility in Yazd and Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak. Day 32: At least 3 projectiles landed near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — IAEA warnings of potential major radiological incident. ~480 Russians on site. Multiple Iranian parliamentarians pushing NPT withdrawal legislation. IAEA has had no access to any of Iran's four declared enrichment facilities since June 2025. Iran possesses 440.9 kg of uranium enriched to 60% — enough for ~9-10 nuclear weapons if further enriched. Day 33: Three projectiles have struck near Bushehr NPP, closest landing 350 meters from the operating reactor (Mar 17). Plant holds 72 tons of fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel. Rosatom CEO Likhachev warned any direct hit could cause 'a regional-scale disaster.' IAEA DG Grossi called Bushehr 'the reddest line of all.' Iran's parliament pushing to exit the Non-Proliferation Treaty. Natanz entrance buildings inaccessible. Fordow significantly damaged from June 2025 strikes. ~480 Russian Rosatom employees remain at Bushehr; third evacuation being prepared.

label: Airports Damaged
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. Regional airspace closures affect 24,000+ commercial flights.

label: Bridges Destroyed
note: Western Iran + all Litani River bridges in Lebanon destroyed
detail: Iran: Road and rail bridges in Kermanshah, Lorestan, and Khuzestan provinces destroyed to degrade IRGC logistics. Lebanon: IDF claims all bridges over Litani destroyed. Israel declares 'security zone' south of Litani River (~30 km) — DM Katz says displaced Lebanese 'will not return south of the Litani.' Defense Minister Katz ordered demolition of border villages using 'Beit Hanoun and Rafah models.'
